claim: The implementation's curvature metric is a discrete polyhedral face-path turn metric, not the Julia-Caratheodory angular derivative.
Cut-and-project schemes generate lower-dimensional model sets or tilings from a lattice in a product of direct and internal spaces.
Geometry of Interaction gives a computation model where execution follows paths/trips through graph-like proof structures.
Polyhedral graphs are closely related to 3D topology and geometry and can be treated as planar 3-connected graph structures.
The SCBE higher-shape implementation executes a bounded tape-machine model, not a physical optical computer.
